faqs.q: How much does tree removal cost in McKinney, TX?
faqs.a: Tree removal in McKinney typically ranges from $400 to $2,500 depending on tree size, location, and accessibility. Small trees under 30 ft average $400–$700, while large oaks or elms over 60 ft run $1,200–$2,500. We provide free on-site estimates.

faqs.q: Do you offer emergency tree service in McKinney?
faqs.a: Yes — we offer 24/7 emergency tree service throughout McKinney and Collin County. Storm damage, fallen trees, and hazard removal handled around the clock. Call (972) area number anytime.

faqs.q: Is tree removal covered by homeowners insurance in Texas?
faqs.a: Most Texas homeowners policies cover tree removal when a fallen tree damages a structure. Removal of a standing hazard tree is typically not covered unless it has already caused damage. We work with adjusters and can provide documentation.

faqs.q: Do you service Frisco, Allen, and Plano in addition to McKinney?
faqs.a: Yes — we serve the entire Collin County area including Frisco, Allen, Plano, Prosper, Celina, Anna, and surrounding communities.

faqs.q: How long does tree removal take?
faqs.a: Most residential tree removals take 2–6 hours. Larger or complex jobs may take a full day. We handle all cleanup and debris removal.
